page: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a General Purpose server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 16 vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Storage is network-based with a capacity of 1 GB, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier (top 10%). However, multi-core performance metrics, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, fall into the average category (middle 50%). This variance is likely influenced by the shared CPU allocation. Memory performance is robust, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, while uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth remain in the middle 50%.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its shared CPU allocation, which can lead to variable multi-core performance under sustained heavy loads, despite the high-performing AMD EPYC hardware. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high density of memory and vCPUs relative to its resource class, making it a viable option for workloads that benefit from high memory capacity and strong single-threaded performance without requiring dedicated CPU cores. Recommended workloads include static web serving, Redis caching, database operations, and lightweight LLM text generation using small models, where it demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds.
description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a General Purpose server instance from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture, it utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. The instance features 1 GB of network-based local storage. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and small-model LLM text generation speed, while multi-core metrics and memory bandwidth align with average tiers. The PassMark memory score is strong, placing in the top 25%. Due to the shared CPU architecture, multi-core performance may experience variability under sustained loads. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-dense workloads, making it suitable for static web serving, Redis caching, database operations, and lightweight text generation tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-16c-64g is a General Purpose instance featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage, offering top-tier single-core performance and strong memory capacity.

tagline: A memory-dense shared instance delivering top-tier single-core performance and balanced resources for database operations and web serving.

Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
